an11.16:1.1: At one time, Venerable Ānanda was dwelling at Vesālī, in the Beluva village.
an11.16:1.2: Now at that time, Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara had arrived at Pāṭaliputta on some business or other.
an11.16:10.1: When this was said, Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara said to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:10.2: "Just as, *bhante* Ānanda, a man searching for one treasure-door might at once find eleven treasure-doors,
an11.16:10.3: so too, *bhante*, I, searching for one door to the deathless, have at once obtained eleven doors to the deathless to resort to.
an11.16:10.4: Just as, *bhante*, a man's house has eleven doors, and if that house caught fire he could make himself safe through any one of those doors —
an11.16:10.5: so too, *bhante*, I shall be able to make myself safe through any one of these eleven doors to the deathless.
an11.16:10.6: Even those of other sects will seek a teacher's fee for their teacher.
an11.16:10.7: How then could I not pay honour to Venerable Ānanda?"
an11.16:11.1: Then Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara had the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* from Vesālī and from Pāṭaliputta assembled, and with his own hands served them and satisfied them with fine food, both soft and hard.
an11.16:11.2: He clothed each *bhikkhu* individually with a pair of robes, and Venerable Ānanda with three robes.
an11.16:11.3: And he had a monastery of five hundred rooms built for Venerable Ānanda.
an11.16:2.1: Then Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara went to the Kukkuṭa monastery and approached a certain *bhikkhu*; having approached, he said to that *bhikkhu*:
an11.16:2.2: "Where, *bhante*, is Venerable Ānanda dwelling at present?
an11.16:2.3: For we wish to see Venerable Ānanda, *bhante*."
an11.16:2.4: "That Venerable Ānanda, householder, is dwelling at Vesālī, in the Beluva village."
an11.16:3.1: Then Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara, having concluded that business in Pāṭaliputta, went to Vesālī, to the Beluva village, and approached Venerable Ānanda; having approached, he paid homage to Venerable Ānanda and sat down to one side. Seated to one side, Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanāgara said to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:3.2: "Is there, *bhante* Ānanda, one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the fully awakened one — such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, the mind [*citta*] not yet liberated becomes liberated, the *āsavas* not yet destroyed come to destruction, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?"
an11.16:3.3: "There is, householder, one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the fully awakened one — such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, the mind [*citta*] not yet liberated becomes liberated, the *āsavas* not yet destroyed come to destruction, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained."
an11.16:4.1: "And what, *bhante* Ānanda, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the fully awakened one — such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, the mind [*citta*] not yet liberated becomes liberated, the *āsavas* not yet destroyed come to destruction, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?"
an11.16:4.2: "Here, householder, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome *dhammas*, enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicara*], and the *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*viveka*].
an11.16:4.4: 'This first *jhāna* too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:4.5: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:4.6: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:4.7: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:4.8: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the fully awakened one — such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, the mind [*citta*] not yet liberated becomes liberated, the *āsavas* not yet destroyed come to destruction, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:5.1: Furthermore, householder, with the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicara*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, which has internal clarity and unification [*ekodibhūtā*] of mind [*citta*], is without thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicara*], and has *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* …
an11.16:5.3: enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an11.16:5.5: 'This fourth *jhāna* too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:5.6: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:5.7: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:5.8: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:5.9: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the fully awakened one — such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, the mind [*citta*] not yet liberated becomes liberated, the *āsavas* not yet destroyed come to destruction, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:6.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu* dwells pervading one direction with a mind [*citta*] imbued with *mettā*, and likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, and everywhere, to all as to himself, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind [*citta*] imbued with *mettā*, vast, exalted, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an11.16:6.3: 'This *mettā* liberation of mind [*citta*] too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:6.4: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:6.5: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:6.6: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:6.7: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees … the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:7.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu* dwells with a mind [*citta*] imbued with *karuṇā* …
an11.16:7.10: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees … the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:7.3: dwells pervading one direction with a mind [*citta*] imbued with *upekkhā*, and likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
an11.16:7.4: Thus above, below, across, and everywhere, to all as to himself, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind [*citta*] imbued with *upekkhā*, vast, exalted, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an11.16:7.6: 'This *upekkhā* liberation of mind [*citta*] too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:7.7: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:7.8: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:7.9: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:8.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of perceptions of form [*rūpa*], with the passing away of perceptions of resistance, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity, aware that 'space is infinite', enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite space [*āyatana*].
an11.16:8.3: 'This attainment of the dimension of infinite space [*āyatana*] too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:8.4: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:8.5: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:8.6: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:8.7: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees … the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:9.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of the dimension of infinite space [*āyatana*], aware that '*viññāṇa* is infinite', enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*āyatana*] …
an11.16:9.2: with the complete transcending of the dimension of infinite *viññāṇa* [*āyatana*], aware that 'there is nothing', enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*āyatana*].
an11.16:9.4: 'This attainment of the dimension of nothingness [*āyatana*] too is constructed [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*] and intentionally produced.'
an11.16:9.5: 'Whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccā*], subject to cessation' — he understands this.
an11.16:9.6: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:9.7: and if he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, that delight in *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who spontaneously reappears there, and there attains final *nibbāna*, not returning from that world.
an11.16:9.8: This too, householder, is that one *dhamma* well proclaimed by that *Bhagavā* who knows and sees … the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained."
